question:
Following are the intrusive landforms of volcanic activity and their characteristic features :
I. Batholiths : Huge mass of igneous rocks, usually granite at the deeper layers of the crust.
II. Phacoliths : Large igneous mounds with a dome-shaped upper surface.
III. Dykes : Wall-like structures developed by solidifying almost perpendicular to the ground.
IV. Sills : The near horizontal bodies of intrusive igneous rocks.
Which of the above are correctly matched ?

Consider the following statements :
I. Silicon and oxygen are common elements in all types of feldspar and sodium, potassium, calcium, aluminium etc. are found in specific feldspar variety.
II. Almost one-fourth of the Earth's crust is composed of feldspar and quartz.
Which of the above statements is/are correct ?

options.1: Geomorphic processes bring about changes in the configuration of the surface of the Earth.
options.2: Diatrophism and volcanism are endogenic geomorphic processes.
options.3: The endogenic and exogenic forces cause physical stresses and chemical actions on earth materials.
options.4: Orogeny is the continental building process.
